name: Pumpkin & Broccoli Chowder
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons Butter or mild vegetable oil
1 tablespoon Tamari or shoyu soy sauce
4 cups Fresh pumpkin or butternut squash puree (or use canned)
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 bunch Broccoli, tops cut into small florets, stems julienned into strips
recipeInstructions:
(From "Soup and Bread: A Country Inn Cookbook" by Crescent Dragonwagon. Workman Publishing; $12.95)
1. In a 10-inch skillet, melt the butter or heat the oil over medium-low heat.
2. Add the onion and sauté slowly until almost limp but not brown, about 6-7 minutes.
3. Add the tamari or soy sauce and tomato. Cook, stirring often, until the tomato's juice has evaporated, about 6 minutes.
4. Transfer the saute to a soup pot. Deglaze the saute pan with a little stock, and add the pan contents plus the remainder of the stock to the soup pot. Add the pumpkin or squash puree, maple syrup or honey, salt, and a touch of freshly ground black pepper. Heat, stirring often.
5. Separately, in a small pot with a tightly fitting lid, steam the broccoli florets and stems until tender-crisp (they should be bright green), about 3-4 minutes.
6. Stir the steamed broccoli into the soup, then stir in the cream. Let the soup cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until hot and the flavors have blended, 8-10 minutes.
